non_claims: no automatic backup or migration recovery
no recovery after lost mount credential
no full power-loss or FUSE-daemon crash certification

policy_rows.current_evidence: The retained provisioning probe observes NV index 0x01500010 with ownerread|ownerwrite attributes, and pqc_anchor.c uses the TPM owner hierarchy for nvread/nvwrite.
policy_rows.intended_policy: The prototype treats the NV index as an administrator-owned freshness register.  It reads and writes only the authenticated prefix anchor record and does not claim a deployed TPM authorization policy.

policy_rows.current_evidence: The transient PCR probe unseals under the current PCR file and rejects a drifted PCR file; it does not provision the persistent filesystem NV index.
policy_rows.intended_policy: PCR binding is a future release gate for stronger claims, not a property of the current persistent filesystem anchor.
policy_rows.paper_gate_phrase: PCR binding is a transient probe rather than a persistent filesystem policy

policy_rows.current_evidence: pqc_anchor.c checks tpm2_nvreadpublic and refuses to define an index implicitly; the unprovisioned TPM artifact records a nonzero hardware-anchor startup exit.
policy_rows.intended_policy: Administrators create and rotate the NV index outside the daemon; the daemon fails closed when the configured index is absent.
policy_rows.paper_gate_phrase: administrators create and rotate the index outside the daemon

policy_rows.current_evidence: The implementation has no automatic NV define/redefine path and no artifact showing safe automatic anchor migration.
policy_rows.intended_policy: Reprovisioning is a deliberate administrative reset or migration step that must be paired with a compatible storage snapshot or a fresh anchor bootstrap.

policy_rows.current_evidence: PCR drift is rejected only in the transient PCR-policy probe; no persistent PCR-sealed filesystem anchor is installed.
policy_rows.intended_policy: Software updates or PCR changes require explicit policy renewal before the paper can claim persistent PCR-bound freshness.
policy_rows.paper_gate_phrase: software update or PCR change therefore requires explicit reprovisioning/resealing

policy_rows.current_evidence: The monotonic replay and combined SQLite/dbm.dumb campaigns show that stale backing-store snapshots fail closed against the advanced TPM anchor.
policy_rows.intended_policy: Backup and migration are not blind directory copies; a destination needs an authorized anchor bootstrap or the restored state should fail closed.
policy_rows.paper_gate_phrase: Backup or migration cannot copy only the backing directory

policy_rows.current_evidence: The paper and code keep the mount password as the root credential; no hardware-backed credential release or recovery artifact exists.
policy_rows.intended_policy: Lost mount credentials are unrecoverable in this prototype.

policy_rows.current_evidence: The source fails closed on missing NV index, TPM I/O failure, anchor digest mismatch, and stored sequence ahead of local state; retained replay artifacts expose fail-closed read/open behavior.
policy_rows.intended_policy: Missing index, authorization failure, TPM I/O failure, digest mismatch, or a TPM sequence ahead of local state must fail closed instead of exposing stale data.
policy_rows.paper_gate_phrase: Missing index, authorization failure, TPM I/O failure, digest mismatch, or a TPM sequence ahead of local state fail closed

scope: Policy-definition artifact only.  It records the intended TPM/freshness lifecycle and gates paper wording; it does not claim persistent PCR-bound freshness or deployed credential recovery.
